Alma 24:29 raises the possibility of still another group being present. It says that among those converted by the Nephite missionaries, "there were none who were [1] Amalekites or [2] Amulonites or [3] who were of the order of Nehor, but they [the converts] were actual descendants of Laman and Lemuel." This phrasing leaves unclear whether those "of the order of Nehor" were merely Amalekites or Amulonites who followed the Nehorite persuasion, or whether, as seems equally likely, the Nehorites…

Taken away the guilt... through the merits of his Son] Because of the atonement of Christ, the truly repentant have not only the burden of sin lifted from them but also the burden of guilt. Through the Atonement we become yoked to Christ; he thereby shares our burden. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 3:, p.168)
